visa_types.name: Airport Transit
visa_types.documents_required:
Checklist for Airport Transit. (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/norway/lebanon/english/pdf/Checklist-for-Airport-Transit-New.pdf)
- Notes:
- You cannot work while you are visiting Norway/Schengen on a visitor’s visa.
- You cannot get a visitor’s visa to apply for a residence permit in Norway/Schengen area.
- A visa is only intended for short-term stays in the Schengen region and not for permanent or long-term residency.
- If you attempt to use a visa stay in Norway or another Schengen country to obtain permanent or long-term residency you can be given a penalty period. This means that you would not normally be able to obtain a visa to enter Schengen countries during that period.
visa_types.visa_fees:
For VISA FEE LIST please click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598)
The visa fee is subject to change without notice.
Please Note: 
- The Visa fee will be paid online at Norway portal here (https://www.udi.no/en/).
- The detailed list of visa fees at Norway Portal is a click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3592).
- Service fee of 15 euro will be paid in cash at the visa application center
- The visa application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[Table:Tourist]
Courier re-submission:
The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
- VFS service charges and all optional services are to be paid in cash only in Euro.
- Fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time: The processing IS 15 days starting from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application, also refer to UDI website: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319 (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319)
visa_types.overview:
Overview

- A visitor's visa allows you to stay in Norway for up to 90 days over a period of 180 days
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

- A visitor's visa allows you to stay in Norway for up to 90 days over a period of 180 days

## Visa Fees
For VISA FEE LIST please click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598)
The visa fee is subject to change without notice.
Please Note: 
- The Visa fee will be paid online at Norway portal here (https://www.udi.no/en/).
- The detailed list of visa fees at Norway Portal is a click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3592).
- Service fee of 15 euro will be paid in cash at the visa application center
- The visa application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[Table:Tourist]
Courier re-submission:
The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
- VFS service charges and all optional services are to be paid in cash only in Euro.
- Fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Checklist for Airport Transit. (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/norway/lebanon/english/pdf/Checklist-for-Airport-Transit-New.pdf)
- Notes:
- You cannot work while you are visiting Norway/Schengen on a visitor’s visa.
- You cannot get a visitor’s visa to apply for a residence permit in Norway/Schengen area.
- A visa is only intended for short-term stays in the Schengen region and not for permanent or long-term residency.
- If you attempt to use a visa stay in Norway or another Schengen country to obtain permanent or long-term residency you can be given a penalty period. This means that you would not normally be able to obtain a visa to enter Schengen countries during that period.

## Photo Specifications
The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
the photo must be:
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note! If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
Photo/passport photo - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/) 
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child.

## Processing Time
The processing IS 15 days starting from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application, also refer to UDI website: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319 (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319)

## Download Forms
Visa Application Form (https://my.udi.no/) 

## To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

- You cannot get a visitor’s visa to apply for a residence permit in Norway/Schengen area.
- A visa is only intended for short-term stays in the Schengen region and not for permanent or long-term residency.
- If you attempt to use a visa stay in Norway or another Schengen country to obtain permanent or long-term residency you can be given a penalty period. This means that you would not
                                                                            normally be able to obtain a visa to enter Schengen countries during that period.

- The detailed list of visa fees at Norway Portal is a click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3592)

- Service fee of 15 euro will be paid in cash at the visa application center
- The visa application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[Table:Tourist]
Courier re-submission:
The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
- VFS service charges and all optional services are to be paid in cash only in Euro.
- Fees are non-refundable.

- The visa application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[Table:Tourist]
Courier re-submission:
The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.

- Fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required

Checklist for family members of EU/EEA citizens.
Notes:
- You cannot work while you are visiting Norway/Schengen on a visitor’s visa.
- You cannot get a visitor’s visa to apply for a residence permit in Norway/Schengen area.
- A visa is only intended for short-term stays in the Schengen region and not for permanent or long-term residency.
- If you attempt to use a visa stay in Norway or another Schengen country to obtain permanent or long-term residency you can be given a penalty period. This means that you would not
                                                                            normally be able to obtain a visa to enter Schengen countries during that period.

## Photo Specifications
The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
the photo must be:
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note! If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
Photo/passport photo - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/) 
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child.

## Processing Time
The processing is 15 days starting from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application, also refer to UDI website: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319 (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319)

## Download Forms
Visa Application Form (https://my.udi.no/) 

## To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
 This category is for persons who are applying for Study, Work and other permits in Norway. 
Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
For information about studies in Norway, please refer to www.studyinnorway.no (https://www.studyinnorway.no). To find out more about regulations concerning residence permit for students, you should visit the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration at www.udi.no/templates/Tema.aspx?id=7415 (https://www.udi.no/templates/Tema.aspx?id=7415)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
 This category is for persons who are applying for Study, Work and other permits in Norway. 
Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
For information about studies in Norway, please refer to www.studyinnorway.no (https://www.studyinnorway.no). To find out more about regulations concerning residence permit for students, you should visit the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration at www.udi.no/templates/Tema.aspx?id=7415 (https://www.udi.no/templates/Tema.aspx?id=7415)

## Visa Fees
For VISA FEE LIST please click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598)
The visa fee is subject to change without notice.
Please Note: 
- The Visa fee will be paid online at Norway portal here (https://www.udi.no/en/)
- The detailed list of visa fees at Norway Portal is a click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3592)
- Service fee of 15 euro will be paid in cash at the visa application center
- The visa application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[Table:Tourist]
Courier re-submission:
The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
- VFS service charges and all optional services are to be paid in cash only in Euro.
- Fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please check the requirements that mentioned on the application, all certificates must be translated into English and verified by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the issuing country.
All documents must be A4, with no staples, no stickers, no torn pages.
                                                                        

## Applications for work permit in Norway:
For more information about work permits in Norway, please consult to the Norwegian Immigration Office's information on the subject here:
Work permit in Norway  (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Central-topics/Work-and-residence/)
Please fill in the application form (https://my.udi.no) (UDI Application Portal Norway) and pay the fees. There is no need for making appointment in advance, please check opening hours (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/lbn/en/nor/attend-centre/Beirut).
Checklists which explain which documents you must hand in with your application (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)

## Photo Specifications
The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
the photo must be:
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note! If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child.
Photo/passport photo - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/)

## Processing Time
The processing time at (UDI) The Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)

## Download Forms
https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/forms/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/forms/)

## To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

- A visitor's visa allows you to stay in Norway for up to 90 days over a period of 180 days

## Visa Fees
For VISA FEE LIST please click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598)
The visa fee is subject to change without notice.
Please Note: 
- The Visa fee will be paid online at Norway portal here (https://www.udi.no/en/).
- The detailed list of visa fees at Norway Portal is a click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3592).
- Service fee of 15 euro will be paid in cash at the visa application center
- The visa application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[Table:Tourist]
Courier re-submission:
The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
- VFS service charges and all optional services are to be paid in cash only in Euro.
- Fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required

Checklist for Medical Treatment. 

Notes:
- You cannot work while you are visiting Norway/Schengen on a visitor’s visa.
- You cannot get a visitor’s visa to apply for a residence permit in Norway/Schengen area.
- A visa is only intended for short-term stays in the Schengen region and not for permanent or long-term residency.
- If you attempt to use a visa stay in Norway or another Schengen country to obtain permanent or long-term residency you can be given a penalty period. This means that you would not normally be able to obtain a visa to enter Schengen countries during that period.

## Photo Specifications
The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
the photo must be:
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note! If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
Photo/passport photo - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/) 
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child.

## Processing Time
The processing IS 15 days starting from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application, also refer to UDI website: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319 (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319)

## Download Forms
Visa Application Form (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/forms/)

## To Download a Form:
- https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/forms/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/forms/)
- Click on the
 
form
 
link
- When the form opens, save the form on your
 
computer.
OR
- Press the right button on
 
your mouse
 
on the required link.
- Select
 
Save Target As
 
from the list
- Save
 
the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: Family Immigration
visa_types.documents_required:
- Gather all the documents in the relevant checklist on UDI’s website (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)
- Please check this list (https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf) for which documents on the checklist (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/) that must be legalized and/or submitted in original. The list also has the names of these documents in Arabic.
- The applicant must bring the original documents and the passport. These documents will be checked by the embassy and will be returned to the applicant later
- The original documents must be legalized by MFA of issuing country. No legalization of the passport or ID is required.
- The applicant must also bring copies of all pages of the documents and passport.
- If the documents are in a different language than Norwegian or English, they must be translated to Norwegian or English by an authorized/certified translator. It must be stated on the translation who translated the document and when

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

## Visa Fees
For VISA FEE LIST please click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598)
The visa fee is subject to change without notice.
Please Note: 
- The Visa fee will be paid online at Norway portal here (https://www.udi.no/en/).
- The detailed list of visa fees at Norway Portal is a click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3592).
- Service fee of 15 euro will be paid in cash at the visa application center
- The visa application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[Table:Tourist]
Courier re-submission:
The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
- VFS service charges and all optional services are to be paid in cash only in Euro.
- Fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
- Gather all the documents in the relevant checklist on UDI’s website (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)
- Please check this list (https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf) for which documents on the checklist (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/) that must be legalized and/or submitted in original. The list also has the names of these documents in Arabic.
- The applicant must bring the original documents and the passport. These documents will be checked by the embassy and will be returned to the applicant later
- The original documents must be legalized by MFA of issuing country. No legalization of the passport or ID is required.
- The applicant must also bring copies of all pages of the documents and passport.
- If the documents are in a different language than Norwegian or English, they must be translated to Norwegian or English by an authorized/certified translator. It must be stated on the translation who translated the document and when

## Photo Specifications
The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
the photo must be:
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note! If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
Photo/passport photo - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/) 
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child.

## Processing Time
The processing time at (UDI) The Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)

## Download Forms

## To Download a Form:
- https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/forms/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/forms/)
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
